methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $3.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: xAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $250B

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: Groq
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $7B

private.name: SandboxAQ
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.6B

private.name: WEKA

private.name: Modal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.1B

data.narrative:
Ayar Labs, an AI & Software company at Series E, is valued at $3.75B post-money following $500M in total capital raised. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of six same-stage, same-sector private companies (xAI, Crusoe Energy, Groq, SandboxAQ, WEKA, and one additional peer) plus one relevant public comparable (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement to the $3.75B last post-money valuation, implying $5.2B. Peer-median methodology across the six private comparables yields a median valuation of $6.3B, though with material dispersion: range of $1.1B to $250.0B reflects significant heterogeneity within the AI & Software cohort, driven primarily by xAI's $250B valuation outlier.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $4.5B (low) to $7.2B (high), with a median point of $5.8B. This band reflects the current $3.75B valuation as below midpoint, consistent with typical pre-exit compression in Series E rounds. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.4x stage multiplier given sector volatility, the outsized influence of xAI on peer medians, and whether public market comparables (PLTR) offer sufficient operational similarity. Tighter confidence attaches to the $5.2B–$6.3B interval.
